entry.title: Be Part of Tomorrow: Join SAP's Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1, 2025!
entry.content:
We’re thrilled to announce the launch of the first cycle of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2025, featuring 50 exciting new projects aimed at fostering innovation and collaboration with our valued SAP customers and partners.

In today’s fast-paced business world, staying ahead is more important than ever. The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative presents a unique opportunity to gain early insights into SAP’s product developments. By participating, you’ll work directly with our developers and product managers, helping shape the future of our products.

Register until March 28, 2025! Don’t miss out on the opportunity to have your voice heard and influence the next generation of SAP solutions.

To help you explore the available projects, we’ve compiled a detailed list for your convenience. Check out the full list here or follow the links below to discover the exciting opportunities:

ABAP

  • ABAP Cloud Development Assistant
  • OData UI Service from scratch Generator

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • BTP AI based Data Anonymization Service
  • Create AI-driven extensions and automations with SAP Build
  • Improving the Admin's and Solution Architect's Experience on SAP BTP
  • SAP BPM, SAP BRM and SAP Process Orchestration migration to Cloud
  • SAP BTP Cockpit, Cloud Foundry UIs
  • SAP BTP Usability Event - User research project
  • SAP Business Technology Platform: Multi-Region Desaster Recovery
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Provisioning, Integration with Joule 
  • SAP Integration Suite

SAP Concur

  • SAP Concur - AI Managed Expense Reports
  • SAP Concur - Dynamic Card Management – Virtual Credit Cards
  • SAP Concur Expense - Web Services for Financial Integration
  • SAP Concur Mobile app and the sign-in user experience
  • SAP Concur Solutions – All About E-Receipts
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Customer Managed Keys for granular control over data
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Sponsored Guest Feature
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Web Notifications Center and Preferences

SAP Customer Experience

  • Research project into a potential loyalty cloud solution
  • SAP CX AI Toolkit

Data & Analytics

  • Redesigning the Dashboard Experience
  • SAP HANA Cloud Elastic Compute Node
  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • Bank Relationship Management: Improving Overview, Services & Fee, and Interest Calculations
  • Fiori First for Sales Order Management
  • Identifying and resolving critical situations in a production planners day in S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • Identity and Access Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Insights into Business processes with Business Event Logging
  • Joule and GenAI use cases in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud
  • New Financial Reporting for Enterprise Project (Project Booklet) including ETO
  • Planned New Solution for Cost Component Breakdown
  • Project staffing, consider dependencies across different roles in a project
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Product Change Management
  • Simplification of transports in SAP S/4HANA® Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape
  • Usability Improvements for managing attachments in S/4HANA business applications

SAP Globalization

  • Australia and New Zealand: GST requirement for Down payments
  • Australia: Modified Payment Times Report - AASB 8 and Taxable Payments Annual Report
  • ISO 21378 Audit Data Collection Standard
  • Planned enhancements to Run statutory reports to handle Draft Report reconciliation
  • Sales and Use Tax Reporting in United States - User Research
  • Sales and Use Tax payments in United States

Lifecycle Management

  • DevOps with ABAP

SAP Integration Experience Feedback goes SAP Customer Engagement Initiative

  • Assisted Assessment of Aligned Business APIs for External Workforce
  • Assisted Assessment of Aligned Business APIs for Hire to Retire Cloud
  • Assisted Assessment of Aligned Business APIs for Source-to-Pay

Others

  • Research into AI-Assisted User Learning Rollout for Adoption
  • SAP Reference Architecture in SAP LeanIX
  • Shape the future of SAP Cloud ALM Configuration & Security Analysis
  • The AI-Driven Future of New Product Introduction (NPI)

We believe that collaboration is key to sparking innovation. By working alongside our customers and partners, we can jointly shape the future of SAP’s products and transform business operations.

Let’s take this journey together. Together, we have the power to drive progress and transform industries.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2025!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Unlocking Business Opportunities at Siemens through the Power of Localization and Clean Core
entry.content:
As a global business leader, navigating the complexities of local regulations and market specificities while maintaining a large-scale operation can be daunting. At SAP, we have seen the transformative power of localization — the strategic adaptation of business processes to meet local needs. Today, I want to share how Siemens is leveraging SAP's localization capabilities to streamline its global operations. We had the pleasure of speaking with two seasoned professionals at Siemens: Ms. Andrea Spandau and Mr. Uwe Stuerzenhofecker, experienced transformation leaders in the area of Tax and Customs processes. Their insights provide a deeper understanding of how the company is navigating this transformative process and the critical role of localization in ensuring global success.

Driving Enterprise Success - The Siemens Journey

Siemens serves various sectors, including industry, infrastructure, mobility, and healthcare. As a global powerhouse operating in more than 170 countries and boasting a rich history of innovation and reliability over its 175-year journey, Siemens has consistently adapted to the shifting business landscapes of digitalization and sustainability. The implementation of a clean core ERP system has been a game-changer. By aligning business processes with strategy and maintaining global standards, Siemens ensures lean operations and enhanced focus on delivering customer-centric solutions. This approach not only streamlines internal processes but significantly boosts customer satisfaction as clients experience tailored services that genuinely meet their local needs.

A key aspect of Siemens' transformation is the SHERPA X program, which aims to migrate the company's ERP systems to SAP S/4HANA. This ambitious project involves more than 1,000 legal entities across 60 different ERP systems, with SAP being the primary platform.

The Strategic Role of Harmonization and Localization

Localization is an integral part of the Siemens SAP S/4HANA transformation program. Siemens recognizes that a global template does not fit every country, and local legal requirements must be met. The localization process starts with deciding which country will be localized, followed by the creation of a country-specific blueprint that serves as a basis for future businesses operating in that country.

Siemens has a dedicated localization team that collaborates with regional experts to conduct fit-gap analyses and ensure that the template covers all legal requirements. This process involves a series of workshops and deep-dive sessions, engaging local experts to virtually discuss local legal requirements to be implemented for the end-to-end processes such as lead to cash, purchase to pay and record to report.

Siemens faces the challenge of aligning its vast operations across different regulatory, tax, and business landscapes. The solution? A robust localization strategy encompassed within their Siemens SAP S/4HANA transformation program. Here’s how they do it:

  1. Developing Country-Specific Blueprint: Starting with a baseline blueprint that encompasses all legal requirements of a country, Siemens crafts a foundational layer essential for compliance. This strategic move allows the company to confidently and swiftly enter new markets.
  2. Incorporating Business-Specific Adaptations: On top of this compliance layer, Siemens integrates specific business processes that are crucial for meeting the localized demands. This dual approach ensures that while the global standards are met, the solutions resonate well with local customers. Harmonization, in Siemens’ strategy, means standardizing core processes across the company while ensuring flexibility. The Siemens SAP S/4HANA transformation program focuses on creating a lean ERP core where indispensable functionalities reside, simplifying maintenance and integration of new technologies or processes.
  3. Ensuring Integrity and Consistency: A key aspect of Siemens’ strategy includes employing SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) to uphold data integrity across all their ERP systems. With MDG, Siemens streamlines data modifications which are only permissible in the central system, thereby maintaining consistency across all customer transactions and financial processes. This strategic governance assures that regardless of geographical boundaries, every operation within Siemens aligns with the core business standards.

A Beacon for Global Enterprises

One of the guiding principles of Siemens' localization approach is maintaining a lean ERP core. Siemens’ commitment to a clean core and comprehensive localization not only benefits their operation but also empowers their clients.

Siemens employs a range of SAP solutions, such as SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) for e-invoicing, SAP Global Trade Services (GTS) for customs and export control, and SAP Business Integrity Screening. These solutions work in harmony with the ERP system, providing a comprehensive and efficient platform for global operations.

The journey Siemens is on serves as an inspiring blueprint for many other global entities. By sharing their strategies and successes, we aim to embolden more enterprises to embrace and leverage localization in their business transformation endeavors. As we continue partnering with companies across various industries and regions, SAP remains dedicated to offering the essential tools and insights required for mastering the intricacies of global operations.

Summary

Siemens' journey towards digitalization and standardization is a testament to the company's commitment to staying ahead in an ever-evolving business landscape. By leveraging SAP's localization capabilities and maintaining a clean core, Siemens is well-positioned to navigate the complexities of global operations and deliver value to its customers.

As the Tax & Customs team continues to drive the specific localization requirements within the Siemens SAP S/4HANA transformation program, they are not only transforming Siemens' internal processes but also setting an example for other global enterprises looking to streamline their operations. The strategies and practices developed during this journey will undoubtedly serve as a valuable guide for other organizations aiming for global success. Through innovative localization and a focus on clean core principles, Siemens is paving the way for a more efficient, compliant, and customer-centric future.

Learn about Localization Today 

Siemens: How Can Automated Document Processing Reduce Compliance Costs Across the Globe?

Localization Capabilities on sap.com 

Accelerate Global Business Readiness with Localized SAP S/4HANA - SAPinsider

SAP Community for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

SAP Community for Localization 

Regulatory Change Manager: Your Solution to Navigate Regulatory Changes with Ease

entry.title: 統一發票在 SAP ERP 公有雲系統中的實施方案建議
entry.content:
在數位化時代，電子發票已成為企業提升流程效率和合規的重要工具。作為專注於 SAP ERP 公有雲的解決方案架構師，基於豐富的專案參與經驗，本文提供關於電子發票管理的深入洞見，旨在幫助企業用戶充分利用 SAP 產品實現統一發票的數位化管理。

 

電子發票流程的必要性

依據台灣財政部的法規，電子發票已被推薦作為標準格式。SAP ERP 公有雲產品提供強大的支持，成為企業在數位轉型中不可或缺的組成部分。在眾多實施專案中，企業面臨的挑戰通常在於如何有效整合電子發票到現有系統及流程中。為確保從紙質到電子系統的過渡順利，建議採用分階段實施策略。

 

理解並優化現有發票流程

在許多企業中，紙質發票的管理常常帶來操作上的繁瑣以及高錯誤率。當企業客製化的系統流程與功能中涉及紙質發票流程時，可能會遇到以下問題：

  • 整合難度：紙質和電子發票系統的整合可能涉及多重系統之間的信息同步和數據轉換，增加了技術復雜性。
  • 效率低下：處理紙質發票通常需要人工干預，如掃描和手動數據輸入，這可能導致效率降低。
  • 錯誤風險：手動過程中容易出現數據錯誤，可能造成財務報表的不準確。

建議企業選擇分開管理紙質和電子發票，並逐步推廣至全電子化進程。基於對 SAP ERP 公有雲的深入認識，系統中的嚴謹配置和測試至關重要，以免發生發票號碼等關鍵數據整合問題。

 

處理停用的電子計算機統一發票

自2021年以來，電子計算機統一發票已經停止適用，參考台灣當地財政部賦稅署公告。停止適用後，為了管理和查閱歷史交易數據，SAP 系統中仍可能出現與電子計算機發票相關的税碼設定。為了防止錯誤使用停用的税碼，專案顧問需要利用稅條件類型的設定，限制用戶只能選擇當前實際適用的銷項稅碼。將客戶稅分類碼進行調整，如用“E”來對應電子發票税碼，以取代傳統設計的“1”税碼，是一個有效的解決方案。

 

電子發票功能的全面展望

SAP ERP 公有雲提供一整套電子發票標準税碼，以協助企業滿足多樣化的稅務需求：

  • A5：零税率銷售，如出口商品。
  • A6：針對境內一般課稅。
  • A7：境內零稅。
  • A8：境內免稅/免開立電子發票銷售。
  • A9：用於境內特殊稅率。

在設計和實施這些功能時，建議企業激活 5XU 範圍項目，並參考以下的配置指南，以確保最大化利用這一數字化工具：

  • 客戶（銷售）電子發票相關配置指南
  • 電子發票類型與編碼配置指南
  • VAT 申報配置指南
  • 營業場所配置指南

 

預備未來的發票標準

根據電子發票資料交換標準訊息建置指引(MIG) ，從2026年起，電子發票需遵循 MIG4.1 標準。當前，SAP 的系統版本支援 MIG4.0 標準，產品研發團隊已經積極應對更新法規的要求，未來的系統更新已列入日程。建議企業提前規劃，以便順利適應法規變更。

免開立電子發票的場景處理

依照統一發票使用辦法第四條 ，某些免税商品可免開立統一發票。在這些情況下，SAP 標準流程並不會自動阻止電子發票的生成。因此，專案可通過增強 BADI EDOC_TW_IS_RELEVANT 實現客製化邏輯，以條件性地抑制電子發票的生成，從而達到業務需求。

 

多元税碼場景的應對方法

隨著 MIG4.0 標準的啟用，“F0401 平台存證開立發票”可在同一張憑證中包含應稅與免税或零税率的項目。SAP ERP 公有雲自 2502 版本起支持混合税率的電子發票。這意味著，在同一張憑證中可以包含應稅與免税或零税率的項目。企業可以利用此功能實現更多元的發票處理模式。詳細說明參見 Multiple Tax | SAP What's New Viewer。

但若企業管理的需求仍需對不同税率單獨開票，有兩種方案可預期實現：

  • 流程控制：規範操作流程，確保同一銷售訂單僅限使用同一税率，從根源上預防混合税率情況。
  • 技術增強：使用 BADI SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER 實施客製化邏輯，在銷售請款憑證生成時，按稅碼拆分請款憑證。需注意，根據 SAP 的設計，電子發票憑證需與銷售請款憑證保持一一對應關係。詳細參見 Forcing a Billing Document Split。

 

集成加值中心與 VAT 申報

在某些專案中，部分企業選擇透過加值中心進行 VAT 申報，這涉及如何將 SAP 系統的電子發票數據有效推送至加值中心。由於不同加值中心對電子資訊交互的要求略有差異，SAP 無法提供統一的解決方案，因此需客製化開發來匹配不同加值中心的技術需求，確保準確數據傳輸與符合規範。

專案實施可透過銷售流程憑證的相關 CDS 視圖讀取資料，並整合為電子發票申報集成所需的資料格式。以下博客詳細闡述了一則實施實例：嵌入式 SAC 客戶用例分享之加值中心集成（台灣地區本地化需求）。若您期望SAP能通过标准产品内容提供统整的台湾电子发票CDS视图，欢迎您通过贵司的 S-User 访问 Release Unified CDS Views for Taiwan e-Invoice Data Export and Integrations | SAP Customer Influence Portal ，投出您宝贵的一票。



結語

透過這些見解，企業在統一發票的數位化管理旅程中能夠獲得新的視角和實用策略。成功的電子發票應用促進業務流程改進，提升合規性，也是企業數位化中不可忽視的環節。欲進一步詳細了解 SAP 台灣電子發票標準方案，歡迎參考SAP Document Compliance 台灣電子發票解決方案。

entry.title: 台湾地区统一发票在 SAP ERP 公有云系统中的实施方案建议
entry.content:
在数字化时代，电子发票已成为企业提升流程效率和合规的重要工具。作为专注于 SAP ERP 公有云的解决方案架构师，基于丰富的项目参与经验，本文提供关于台湾地区统一发票管理的深入洞见，旨在帮助企业用户充分利用 SAP 产品实现统一发票的数字化管理。

 

电子发票流程的必要性

根据台湾财政部的法规，电子发票已被推荐作为统一发票标准格式。SAP ERP 公有云产品提供强大的支持，成为企业在数字化转型中不可或缺的组成部分。在众多实施项目中，企业面临的挑战通常在于如何有效整合电子发票到现有系统及流程中。为确保从纸质到电子系统的过渡顺利，建议采用分阶段实施策略。

 

理解并优化现有发票流程

在许多企业中，纸质发票的管理常常带来操作上的繁琐以及高错误率。当企业定制化的系统流程与功能中涉及纸质发票流程时，可能会遇到以下问题：

  • 整合难度：纸质和电子发票在系统层面的整合可能涉及多个系统之间的信息同步和数据转换，增加了技术复杂性。
  • 效率低下：处理纸质发票通常需要人工干预，如扫描和手动数据输入，这可能导致效率降低。
  • 错误风险：手动过程中容易出现数据错误，可能导致财务报表的不准确。

建议企业选择分开管理纸质和电子发票，并逐步推广至全电子化进程。基于对 SAP ERP 公有云的深入认识，系统中的严谨配置和测试至关重要，以防止发生发票号码等关键数据整合问题。

 

处理停用的电子计算机统一发票

自2021年以来，电子计算机统一发票已经停止适用，参考台湾当地财政部税务署公告。停止适用后，为了管理和查阅历史交易数据，SAP 系统中仍可能出现与电子计算机发票相关的税码设置。为了防止错误使用停用的税码，项目顾问需要利用税条件类型的设置，限制用户只能选择当前实际适用的销项税码。将客户税分类码进行调整，如用“E”来对应电子发票税码，以替代传统设计的“1”税码，是一个有效的解决方案。

 

电子发票功能的全面展望

SAP ERP 公有云提供一整套电子发票标准税码，以协助企业满足多样化的税务需求：

  • A5：零税率销售，如出口商品。
  • A6：针对境内一般课税。
  • A7：境内零税。
  • A8：境内免税/免开立电子发票销售。
  • A9：用于境内特殊税率。

在设计和实施这些功能时，建议企业激活 5XU 范围项目，并参考以下的配置指南，以确保最大化利用这一数字化工具：

  • 客户（销售）电子发票相关配置指南
  • 电子发票类型与编码配置指南
  • VAT 申报配置指南
  • 营业场所配置指南

 

预备未来的发票标准

根据电子发票数据交换标准信息构建指引(MIG)，从2026年起，电子发票需遵循 MIG4.1 标准。当前，SAP 的系统版本支持 MIG4.0 标准，产品研发团队已经积极应对更新法规的要求，未来的系统更新已列入日程。建议企业提前规划，以便顺利适应法规变更。

 

免开立电子发票的场景处理

依据统一发票使用办法第四条，某些免税商品可免开立统一发票。在这些情况下，SAP 标准流程并不会自动阻止电子发票的生成。因此，项目可通过增强 BADI EDOC_TW_IS_RELEVANT 实现定制化逻辑，以条件性地抑制电子发票的生成，从而满足业务需求。

 

多元税码场景的应对方法

随着 MIG4.0 标准的启动，“F0401 平台存证开立发票”可在同一张凭证中包含应税与免税或零税率的项目。SAP ERP 公有云自 2502 版本起支持混合税率的电子发票。这意味着，在同一张凭证中可以包含应税与免税或零税率的项目。企业可以利用此功能实现更多样的发票处理模式。详细说明参见 Multiple Tax | SAP What's New Viewer。

但若企业管理的需求仍需对不同税率单独开票，有两种方案可预期实现：

  • 流程控制：规范操作流程，确保同一销售订单仅限使用同一税率，从根源上预防混合税率情况。
  • 技术增强：使用 BADI SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER 实施定制化逻辑，在销售请款凭证生成时，按税码拆分请款凭证。需注意，根据 SAP 的设计，电子发票凭证需与销售请款凭证保持一一对应关系。详细参见 Forcing a Billing Document Split。

 

集成加值中心与 VAT 申报

在某些项目中，部分企业选择通过加值中心进行 VAT 申报，这涉及如何将 SAP 系统的电子发票数据有效推送至增值中心。由于不同加值中心对电子信息交互的要求略有差异，SAP 无法提供统一的解决方案符合所有加值中心的要求，因此需定制化开发以匹配不同加值中心的技术需求，确保准确数据传输与合规。

项目实施可通过销售流程凭证的相关 CDS 视图读取数据，并整合为电子发票申报集成所需的数据格式。以下博客详细阐述了一个实施实例：嵌入式 SAC 客户用例分享之加值中心集成（台湾地区本地化需求）。若您期望 SAP 能通过标准产品内容提供统合的台湾电子发票 CDS 视图，欢迎您通过贵司的 S-User 访问 Release Unified CDS Views for Taiwan e-Invoice Data Export and Integrations | SAP Customer Influence Portal，投出您宝贵的一票。



结语

通过这些洞见，企业在统一发票的数字化管理旅程中能够获得新的视角和实用策略。成功的电子发票应用促进业务流程改进，提升合规性，也是企业数字化中不可忽视的环节。欲进一步详细了解 SAP 台湾电子发票标准方案，欢迎参考SAP Document Compliance 台湾电子发票解决方案。

entry.title: Expert Insights on Adopting Solutions for Taiwan's Government Uniform Invoices (GUI)
entry.content:
In the digital era, electronic invoicing has become an important tool for enterprises to improve process efficiency and compliance. As a solution architect focusing on SAP ERP public cloud, based on rich project participation experience, by this blog I'd like to provide in-depth insights into Taiwan Government Uniform Invoice (GUI) management, aiming to help enterprise users fully utilize SAP products to achieve unified invoice digital management.

 

Necessity of Electronic Invoice Processes

According to Taiwan Ministry of Finance regulations, electronic invoices have been recommended as a standard format. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provide strong support, becoming an indispensable component in the digital transformation of enterprises. In numerous implementation projects, the challenge for enterprises usually lies in effectively integrating electronic invoices into existing systems and processes. To ensure a smooth transition from paper to electronic systems, a phased implementation strategy is recommended.

 

Understanding and Optimizing Existing Invoice Processes

In many enterprises, the management of paper invoices often leads to operational complexity and high error rates. When custom system processes and functions involve paper invoice processes, the following issues may occur:

  • Integration difficulty: Integration of paper and electronic invoice systems may involve information synchronization and data conversion between multiple systems, increasing technical complexity.
  • Low efficiency: Processing paper invoices often requires manual intervention, such as scanning and manual data input, which may reduce efficiency.
  • Error risk: Manual processes are prone to data errors, which may lead to inaccuracies in financial statements.

It is recommended that enterprises manage paper and electronic invoices separately and gradually transition to a fully electronic process. Based on an in-depth understanding of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, meticulous system configuration and testing are crucial to prevent key data integration issues such as invoice numbers.

 

Handling of Disabled Computerized Uniform Invoices

Since 2021, computerized uniform invoices have been discontinued. Refer to Taiwan local Ministry of Finance Taxation Bureau announcement. After discontinuation, for managing and reviewing historical transaction data, SAP systems may still include tax code settings related to computerized invoices. To prevent incorrect use of discontinued tax codes, project consultants need to use tax condition type settings, restricting users to select only currently applicable output tax codes. Adjusting customer tax classification codes, such as using "E" to correspond to electronic invoice tax codes to replace the traditionally designed "1" tax code, is an effective solution.

 

Comprehensive Outlook on Electronic Invoice Functions

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides a complete set of standard electronic invoice tax codes to help enterprises meet diverse tax needs:

  • A5: Zero tax rate sales, such as export goods.
  • A6: General taxation within Taiwan region.
  • A7: Zero tax within Taiwan region.
  • A8: Tax-exempt/sales w/o issuing electronic invoices withinTaiwan region.
  • A9: Used for special tax rates withinTaiwan region.

In designing and implementing these functions, it is recommended that enterprises activate the 5XU scope item and refer to the following configuration guides to maximize the use of this digital tool:

  • Customer (Sales) Electronic Invoice Configuration Guide
  • Electronic Invoice Type and Coding Configuration Guide
  • VAT Reporting Configuration Guide
  • Business Place Configuration Guide

 

Preparing for Future Invoice Standards

According to Electronic Invoice Data Exchange Standard Message Implementation Guide (MIG), from 2026 onwards, electronic invoices must comply with the MIG4.1 standard. The current SAP system version supports MIG4.0 standard, and the product development team is actively responding to the requirements of updated regulations, with future system updates already scheduled. Enterprises are advised to plan ahead to smoothly adapt to regulatory changes.

 

Handling Scenarios Exempt from Issuing Electronic Invoices

According to the 4th item of Taiwan Government Uniform Invoice Usage Regulations, some tax-exempt goods can be exempt from issuing GUI. In these cases, the SAP standard process does not automatically prevent the generation of electronic invoices. Thus, projects can achieve custom logic through enhancing BADI EDOC_TW_IS_RELEVANT to conditionally suppress the generation of electronic invoices, thereby meeting business needs.

 

Handling Mix Tax Codes Scenarios

With the activation of the MIG4.0 standard, "F0401 platform certificate invoice" can contain taxable and tax-exempt or zero-rated items in the same certificate. With the release of 2502, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition supports mixed tax rate electronic invoices. This means that taxable and tax-exempt or zero-rated items can be included in the same invoice. Enterprises can utilize this functionality to achieve more diverse invoice processing modes. For more detailed instructions, refer to Multiple Tax | SAP What's New Viewer.

However, if the enterprise's management needs still require separate invoicing for different tax rates, there are two workaround solutions:

  • Process control: Standardize the business operation process to ensure that the same sales order is limited to using the same tax rate, preventing the situation of mixed tax rates from the source.
  • Technical enhancement: Apply customized logic using BADI SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER to split billing documents by tax code when generating sales billing documents. Note that, according to SAP's design, electronic invoice documents need to maintain a one-to-one correspondence with sales billing documents. For more details, refer to Forcing a Billing Document Split.

 

Integrating Value-added Center with VAT Declaration

In certain projects, some enterprises choose to conduct VAT declaration through value-added centers, which involves how to effectively push electronic invoice data from the SAP system to the value-added center. Since different value-added centers have slightly differing requirements for electronic information interaction, SAP cannot provide a unified solution. Therefore, custom development is required to match the technical needs of different value-added centers, ensuring accurate data transmission and compliance.

Project implementation can read data through the relevant CDS views of the sales/billing documents and integrate them into the data format required for electronic invoice declaration integration. The following blog details an implementation example: Embedded SAC Customer Case Sharing on Value-added Center Integration (Taiwan Region Localization Needs). If you expect SAP to provide unified Taiwan electronic invoice CDS views through standard product content, we welcome you to visit Release Unified CDS Views for Taiwan e-Invoice Data Export and Integrations | SAP Customer Influence Portal with your company's S-User and cast your valuable vote.



Conclusion

Through these insights, enterprises can gain new perspectives and practical strategies in the digital management journey of unified invoices. Successful electronic invoice applications promote business process improvements and enhance compliance, which are crucial aspects of enterprise digitalization. To further understand SAP's Taiwan electronic invoice standard solutions, you are welcome to refer to SAP Document Compliance Taiwan e-Invoice Solution.

entry.title: Are You Ready for the Mandatory Shift to E-invoicing in Belgium?
entry.content:
The world of invoicing is evolving, and e-invoicing is no longer just a trend, it is a legal requirement that businesses must follow.  Also, in Belgium the commitment to implement e-invoicing broadly has become real.  The law for mandatory domestic B2B e-invoicing in Belgium was published in the Belgian Official Journal on February 20, 2024 and will be effective on January 1, 2026.

The shift to mandatory e-invoicing is not just about business efficiency anymore, it is about adhering to stringent tax regulations and ensuring that your invoicing processes are compliant with legislative requirements.  This significant change presents an urgent need for businesses to ensure their processes are well-prepared for the upcoming changes and to define a strong compliance strategy with the right tools.

Why It’s Time to Act Now: The Transition to E-invoicing

The shift from traditional invoicing methods to e-invoicing is not simply a matter of adopting a more efficient tool, it is a matter of staying compliant with tax requirements.  It is essential for companies to realize that digitalization of invoices is no longer optional, and getting ahead of this change will save both time and resources in the long run.  While some companies may have used EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) to enhance business efficiency in the past, e-invoicing is now the legal expectation.

The shift to e-invoicing mandates transcends borders.  A lot of countries already adopted e-invoicing legislation.  Other countries are now taking significant steps forward to the implementation of e-invoicing mandates.  In Belgium, for example, the B2B e-invoicing mandate will enter into force on January 1, 2026.  Many other countries will follow this trend, such as France, Germany, etc.  This means that companies need a solution that can scale to meet global compliance requirements.

How SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Helps You Stay Compliant Globally

The core objective is to make sure businesses are aligned with the requirements set out by governments for electronic invoicing.

This is where SAP Document and Reporting Compliance comes into play.  SAP Document and Reporting Compliance is designed to help businesses navigate the complexities of e-invoicing and ensure seamless compliance across multiple jurisdictions.  With this solution companies can easily send and receive e-invoices, managing this entire process end-to-end with minimal effort.  The system integrates directly into your existing SAP landscape, making the transition as smooth as possible.

With SAP Document and Reporting Compliance there is a global approach for coping with e-invoicing mandates.  Our solution already supports over 55 countries, and many more countries are on the development roadmap, such as Belgium, France, Germany, etc.  This means whether you’re operating in the EU, in Latin America, or beyond, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance  help you to meeting local e-invoicing requirements, no matter where you do business.

Next to the support of e-invoicing mandates, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance also manages regulatory reporting requirements from many countries worldwide.  For Belgium, for instance, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance supports the filing of the VAT returns (periodic VAT return, intra-Community statement and annual sales list), the balance of payments reporting, etc.

By providing this extensive global coverage, SAP ensures your company is always up to date with the latest regulatory requirements.  The solution is built with flexibility in mind, allowing it to handle various invoicing scenarios across different regions.

In addition, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance provides robust features for data validation, error handling, and reporting, further increasing automation and efficiency in your invoicing processes.  The system’s end-to-end capabilities make it the ideal solution for businesses looking to minimize manual work while ensuring they meet complex legal requirements.

Stay Up to Date with Regulatory Change Manager App of SAP

Regulatory requirements are continuously evolving, and it is essential for businesses to stay on top of these changes.  Through the Regulatory Change Manager app (regulatory change manager) you can stay updated with SAP’s analysis of how these changes impact their solutions.

Conclusion: Don’t Wait — Get Ready for the Future of E-invoicing Today

As increasing digitalization in tax administration is reshaping conventional practices and e-invoicing regulations are constantly evolving, now is the time to act.  It is time to equip your company with the right tools and ensure you are fully prepared for the mandatory shift to e-invoicing.  Let SAP Document and Reporting Compliance guide you every step of the way, so you can focus on growing your business, not worrying about compliance.

 

Stay tuned for more in the coming weeks and share any questions you may have in the comments section below!

 

If you want to learn about the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance in your country and engage with SAP experts, join your regional collaboration group.

Check out the SAP Community for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

 

You can find more details on the e-invocing in our dedicated blogs:

  • Revolutionizing Taxation: Navigating VAT in the Digital Age (ViDA)
  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Cloud or On-Premise? Not an “either or” option, but a streamlined solution for electronic compliance!
  • I received an electronic supplier invoice, what now?

entry.title: Get Involved: Help Shape the Future of SAP Globalization in 2025
entry.content:
As businesses expand globally, navigating local regulations, legal mandates, and statutory reporting becomes complex. SAP Globalization is here to help by providing a comprehensive localization solution across SAP’s portfolio, ensuring companies can succeed both locally and globally.

The first cycle of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative, offers you as SAP customers and partners the chance to directly influence future localization features. By joining, you’ll exchange feedback with SAP product management to shape the future of SAP Globalization.

Registration for SAP Globalization projects is open until March 28, 2025. Don’t miss out on this opportunity to help define the future of global business solutions. Follow the links below to discover the exciting opportunities or check out the full list here:
