  • Australia: Modified Payment Times Report - AASB 8 and Taxable Payments Annual Report
  • ISO 21378 Audit Data Collection Standard
  • Planned enhancements to Run statutory reports to handle Draft Report reconciliation
  • Sales and Use Tax Reporting in United States - User Research
  • Sales and Use Tax payments in United States

We believe that collaboration is key to sparking innovation. By working alongside our customers and partners, we can jointly shape the future of SAP’s products and transform business operations.

Let’s take this journey together. Together, we have the power to drive progress and transform industries.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2025!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: SAP ERP公有云台湾本地化-代扣税实施建议
entry.content:
引言：由于SAP ERP公有云标准并没有提供台湾本地化的代扣税功能，本篇博客主要是基于对其他国家的参考来实现的，欢迎各位读者能够提出更多的意见和建议。

目录：

  • 代扣税介绍
  • 过账代扣税的业务场景
  • 系统中配置代扣税
  • 代扣税功能演示
  • 代扣税报表
  • 结论
  • 参考信息

___________________________________________________________________________

  • 代扣税介绍

代扣税扣减由适用国家/地区的法定要求决定，因此纯粹是一个本地化主题。在SAP ERP公有云中，通过范围项目 1J2-高级合规性报表进行处理。

代扣税是在某些国家/地区开始付款流时收取的税。通常，应纳税方不会直接向税务部门缴纳代扣税。当有权扣减代扣税的客户支付应缴纳代扣税的供应商发票时，客户会从付款金额中减去代扣税金额。然后，客户直接向相应的税务部门缴纳代扣税。但对于自代扣例外，在这种情况下，受税款约束的供应商有权直接向相关部门支付税款。

在台湾地区，代扣税是公司代扣的居民或非居民收入付款（付款方）的金额。如果您要支付非本地（国外）供应商，则需要预扣发票金额的一定百分比并向税务局支付税表，以及要支付给国外供应商的余额。

涵盖如下付款：

  • 股息
  • 利息
  • 租赁收入
  • 版税
  • 技术费用、服务付款
  • 工资和薪水
  • 佣金

台湾的代扣税制度适用于居民和非居民所赚取的各种收入，按收入类型和纳税人的居住身份确定税率和规定。每种类型的付款都适用不同的代扣税率，如果台湾与非居民（外国方）是税收居民的国家/地区之间存在双重税收协议，则优惠税率可能适用。下表详细说明了台湾代扣税系统中不同付款的相关表单。

收入类型

居民个人

非居民个人

外国公司

股息

20%

21%

21%

利息

10%

20%

20%

租赁收入

10%

20%

20%

版税

10%

20%

20%

专业服务费

10%

20%(有例外)

20%

薪水和工资

6%-18%

18% (如果<183 天停留)

不适用

佣金

-

20%

20%

参考文末链接【3】

代扣税举例：台湾公司向外国公司采购专业服务费税前金额为 5000 美元，外国公司将承担预扣税，因此，台湾公司只需要向国外公司支付4000美 元，扣除的1000美 元（5000 美元的 20%）应该支付给税务部门。

  • 过账代扣税的业务场景
  1. 根据每个国家/地区的法律要求，在不同阶段计算代扣税并将其过账到相应的代扣税科目。通常，代扣税是在过账付款的同时过账的。换言之，付款（应付账款）或收款（应收账款）将减去代扣税金额。
  2. 在应付账款中，供应商应纳税，公司有义务扣减代扣税并代表供应商将其支付给税务部门。在应收账款中，公司本身需要缴税。与此公司有业务往来的客户扣减代扣税，并代表公司将其支付给税务部门。在这两种情况下，应纳税人/实体的业务伙伴都会扣减税款并将其支付给税务部门。
  3. 在某些国家/地区（例如巴西、菲律宾和西班牙），代扣税可以或必须在过账发票时过账。这意味着应收账款或应付账款金额会减去代扣税金额。
  4. 在某些国家/地区（例如阿根廷和意大利），某些业务交易需要其他类型的过账。代扣税将作为抵销分录过账到费用科目，而不是按代扣税金额减少的付款金额。维护代扣税代码时为此进行相关设置。
  5. 重置已清算项目时，如果清算凭证包含代扣税数据，则会自动冲销。对于含累计的代扣税类型，如果冲销付款凭证，则会调整累计基础金额。
  6. 本博客中，会示例应付发票时点过账代扣税及付款时点过账代扣税这两种场景。

 

  • 系统中配置代扣税

     1.  激活代扣税

要在SAP ERP公有云中使用代扣税（也称为扩展代扣税），必须先将其激活。

2. 定义代扣税代码– 106576

代扣税代码用于标识不同的代扣税类型。

3. 为付款过账定义代扣税类型– 104226

预扣税可以在两个时间点扣除；可以在开票时或付款时扣除。因此，对于此代扣税类型，将创建一个代扣税类型用于付款，另一个用于发票。

4. 为发票过账定义代扣税类型– 104225

预扣税可以在两个时间点扣除；可以在开票时或付款时扣除。因此，对于此代扣税类型，将创建一个代扣税类型用于付款，另一个用于发票。

5. 为公司代码分配代扣税类型– 103914

6. 定义代扣税码- 104227

在代扣税代码中，维护不同的税率并维护对应扣减的基础。

7. 定义代扣税码生效最小/最大限定金额 – 103915

如果需要，可以定义代扣税码生效最小/最大限定金额，如果不指定任何金额，则代表代扣税没有上下限金额的限定。

8. 将代扣税代码分配给代扣税码 – 105553

9. 为代扣税类型分配汇率类型 - 104400

10. 为过账代扣税分配总账科目 – 100297

11. 检查代扣税配置

 

  • 代扣税功能演示

SAP ERP公有云中代扣税也称为扩展代扣税，包含代扣税类型和代扣税代码。代扣税类型表示基本计算规则。代扣税代码代表这些规则的特定功能，尤其是百分率。

如果特定交易需要多种代扣税，则在SAP ERP公有云系统中通过定义多个代扣税类型来涵盖这一点。输入行项目时，可以为每种代扣税类型输入代扣税数据。但请注意，如果发票中有两组或多组代扣税代码和类型，则必须决定所有建议的代扣税代码和类型是否与交易相关。如果不计算和过账某些建议的代扣税，则必须移除与交易无关的代扣税代码。

 

1. 在发票时点过账代扣税

1). 维护业务伙伴主数据

从如上截屏中能够看到供应商代扣税类型设定为发票过账时点生成代扣税行项目，代扣税码为T1即20%的代扣税率。

2). 过账供应商发票

查看模拟生成的财务分录：

查看到生成了代扣税凭证分录，以抵减应付账款金额。

2. 付款时点过账代扣税

1). 维护业务伙伴主数据

从如上截屏中能够看到供应商代扣税类型设定为付款时点生成代扣税行项目，代扣税码为T1即20%的代扣税率。

2). 过账供应商发票

查看模拟生成的财务分录：

3). 对供应商发票付款

模拟付款凭证，可以看到付款时点生成了下图的代扣税行项目。

目前，对于台湾的代扣税过账时点，客户可以根据需求参考应用对应的场景。

 

  • 代扣税报表

对于某些国家/地区，代扣税报表根据从供应商凭证和客户凭证中收集的信息生成，例如已清算发票、供应商贷记凭证、预付定金和已清算贷记凭证。系统生成可提交给政府部门的文本文件。某些国家/地区的凭证和报表合规性(DRC)功能被激活后，可以使用运行法定报表生成代扣税报表。

例如，如下是美国的代扣税报表：

Withholding Tax (1099-MISC) Report for the US

如下是比利时的代扣税报表信息：

Withholding Tax Report in Belgium

但是目前SAP ERP公有云还没有为台湾提供标准的代扣税申报报表，可以使用如下CDS View来获取相应的代扣税信息：

  • I_SupplierWithHoldingTax ：供应商代扣税
  • I_CustomerWithTax ：客户代扣税
  • I_WithholdingTaxCode ：代扣税码
  • I_SuplrInvcHeaderWhldgTaxAPI01 (供应商发票的代扣税)

现在已经有台湾客户发送代扣税申报报表的需求给SAP ERP公有云产品部门，期待未来台湾本地化功能中代扣税申报报表的呈现。客户请求详细请参考如下链接：

Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence

在CE2502版本开始SAP ERP公有云提供了一个新的应用程序“代扣税分析”，该应用程序能够回答一个问题，那就是“如何计算此交易的代扣税？”，如下是德国公司代码的范例：

对于该应用的信息，请参考帮助文档：Withholding Tax Calculation Analysis | SAP Help Portal

然而，该应用暂时不支持台湾地区使用，会提示如下信息：

期待未来该应用可以开放给台湾使用。

 

  • 结论

在台湾地区，代扣税适用于不同收入类型，例如台湾地区内部生成的股息、租金、利息和版税。其重要性在于确保及时征税、简化税务管理、执行遵约。

在每月的第十天之前，代扣代缴税申报方应向财政部缴纳上个月扣缴的税款，并于下一纳税年度的 1 月之前向税务局提交扣缴证明，具体说明上一年缴纳税款和扣缴的税款。如果在 1 月份有连续的公共节假日，则提交扣缴证明的时期应延长至 2 月 5 日。对于向非居民或在 ROC 中没有常设机构的外国企业支付的收入，代扣代缴税代理应在预扣税款后向财政部支付扣缴的税款，并在扣缴后十天内向税务局提交代扣代缴税报表和收据。参考文末链接【4】

与台湾许多税种一样，对税务申报的误解或错过截止期限，都会给在台内外经营的外国公司造成经济损失。

 

  • 参考信息
  1. Help Portal Documentation | Withholding Tax
  2.  Virtual Data Model and CDS Views | Withholding Tax for Supplier Invoice
  3. Taiwan withholding Tax Guide | Tax | SongjerCPA
  4. When shall a withholding agent file returns and make payments of tax withheld?
  5. Withholding Tax (1099-MISC) Report in the US
  6. Withholding Tax Report in Belgium

entry.title: SAP ERP公有雲台灣本土化 - 扣繳稅方案建議
entry.content:
簡介：由於SAP ERP公有雲標準並未提供針對台灣本土化的扣繳稅功能，因此此部落格主要根據來自其他國家的參考內容，歡迎讀者提出其他意見及建議。

目錄：

  • 扣繳稅簡介
  • 過帳扣繳稅的方案
  • 組態系統中的扣繳稅
  • 扣繳稅功能展示
  • 扣繳稅報表
  • 結論
  • 參考資訊

_______________________________________________________________

  • 扣繳稅簡介

由於扣繳稅扣除是根據適用國家的法定需求所規定，因此這純粹是本土化主題。在SAP ERP公有雲中，這會透過範圍項目 1J2- advanced Compliance Reporting 處理。

扣繳稅為部份國家/地區付款流程開始時收取的稅。一般而言，課稅方不會直接向稅捐機構支付扣繳稅。當授權扣除扣繳稅的客戶支付應付扣繳稅的供應商發票時，客戶會從付款金額中扣除扣繳稅額。接著，客戶會直接將扣繳稅支付給個別稅捐機構。然而，針對自行扣繳，視情況而定，供應商有權直接向機關支付稅款。

在台灣，扣繳稅為貴公司扣繳的居民或非居民所得稅 (付款人) 金額。若您正在支付非本地 (國外) 供應商，則必須扣繳發票金額的特定百分比並支付稅務表單給稅捐機關，以及要支付給國外供應商的餘額。

涵蓋下列付款：

  • 股利
  • 利息
  • 租賃收入
  • 版稅
  • 技術費用、服務付款
  • 工資和薪資
  • 佣金

台灣的代扣稅制，適用於居民與非居民所得的各種收入，按納稅人的收入類型和居住狀態確定稅率和規定。不同扣繳稅率適用於每種支付，如果台灣與非居民（外國人）為稅務居民的國家簽訂雙重徵稅協議，則適用優惠稅率。下列表格詳細說明台灣扣繳稅系統中不同付款的相關表單。

收入類型

居民個人

非居民個人

外國公司

股利

20%

21%

21%

利息

10%

20%

20%

租賃收入

10%

20%

20%

版稅

10%

20%

20%

專業費用

10%

20%〈有異常〉

20%

薪資和工資

6%-18%

18% (若 <183 天住宿)

不適用

佣金

-

20%

20%

參考連結 [3]

扣繳稅款範例：台灣企業向稅前5000元的外商購買專業服務，外商將承擔扣繳稅，因此台灣企業只需向外國企業支付4000美元，應向稅務機關繳納1000美元（折合5,000美元20%）的扣除。

  • 過帳扣繳稅的方案
  1. 根據各國家/地區的法律規定，扣繳稅會在不同階段進行計算，並過帳至對應的扣繳稅科目。作為規則，扣繳稅會在過帳付款的同時過帳。換句話說，扣繳稅額會減少付款 (應付帳款) 或收款 (應收帳款)。
  2. 在應付帳款中，供應商可稅，且公司有義務代表供應商扣除扣繳稅並支付給稅捐機構。在應收帳款中，公司本身需要課稅。與此公司進行業務的客戶可扣除扣繳稅，並代表公司支付給稅捐機構。在這兩種情況下，應稅人/實體的業務夥伴皆會扣除稅並支付給稅捐機構。
  3. 在某些國家/地區 (例如巴西、菲律賓和西班牙)，扣繳稅可在過帳發票時或必須過帳。這表示扣繳稅額會減少應收帳款或應付帳款金額。
  4. 在部份國家/地區 (例如阿根廷和義大利)，部份商業交易需要其他類型的過帳。扣繳稅會過帳為費用科目的沖銷分錄，而非扣繳稅金額減少的付款金額。維護扣繳稅碼時，您可進行相關設定。
  5. 重設結清的項目時，若結清文件包含扣繳稅資料，則會自動迴轉。針對含累計的扣繳稅類型，若已迴轉付款文件，則會調整累計基礎金額。
  6. 在此部落格中，範例 A/P 發票中會顯示付款時過帳扣繳稅和過帳扣繳稅的兩個方案。
  • 組態系統中的扣繳稅

1. 啟用扣繳稅 - 104228

若要在SAP ERP公有雲中使用扣繳稅 (也稱為延伸扣繳稅)，您必須先啟用。

2. 定義扣繳稅碼 – 106576

扣繳稅碼用於識別不同的扣繳稅類型。

3. 定義付款過帳的扣繳稅類型 – 104226

扣繳稅可於兩點扣除；可於開立發票時或付款時扣除。因此，針對此扣繳稅類型，系統會針對付款建立一個扣繳稅類型，而另一個用於發票。

4. 定義發票過帳的扣繳稅類型 – 104225

扣繳稅可於兩點扣除；可於開立發票時或付款時扣除。因此，針對此扣繳稅類型，系統會針對付款建立一個扣繳稅類型，而另一個用於發票。

5. 將扣繳稅類型指派給公司代碼 – 103914

6. 定義扣繳稅碼 - 104227

在扣繳稅碼中維護不同稅率，並維護對應扣除的基準。

7. 定義扣繳稅碼有效最小/最大限制金額 – 103915

您可視需要定義扣繳稅碼有效的最小/最大限制金額，或者若未指定金額，則扣繳稅額沒有下限和下限的金額限制。

8. 將扣繳稅碼指派給扣繳稅代碼 – 105553

9. 將匯率類型指派給扣繳稅類型 - 104400

10. 指派過帳扣繳稅的總帳科目 – 100297

11. 檢查扣繳稅組態

①檢查公司代碼

②檢查業務夥伴

③檢查扣繳稅類型

④模擬計算

 

  • 扣繳稅功能展示

SAP ERP公有雲中的扣繳稅也稱為延伸扣繳稅，並包含扣繳稅類型和扣繳稅碼。扣繳稅類型代表基礎計算規則。扣繳稅碼代表這些規則的特定功能，特別是百分比。

若特定交易需要一個以上的扣繳稅，則 SAP 系統中會透過定義數個扣繳稅類型來涵蓋此扣繳稅。輸入細目時，您可以輸入每個扣繳稅類型的扣繳稅資料。請注意，若發票中有兩個或多個扣繳稅代碼和類型集，則必須決定所有建議的扣繳稅碼和類型是否與交易相關。若未計算並過帳部份建議的扣繳稅，則必須移除與交易無關的扣繳稅碼。

1. 於發票點過帳扣繳稅方案

    1) 維護業務夥伴主檔資料

在上述擷取畫面中，您可看到供應商扣繳稅類型已設為發票過帳的時間點，以產生扣繳稅代碼 T1 (扣繳稅率為 20%) 的扣繳稅明細項目。

2). 過帳供應商發票

檢視模擬產生的財務輸入項：

您會看到扣繳稅文件輸入項已產生，以減少應付金額。

2. 於付款點過帳扣繳稅

    1). 維護業務夥伴主檔資料

在上述擷取畫面中，您可看到已將供應商扣繳稅類型設定為付款點，以產生扣繳稅代碼 T1 的扣繳稅明細項目 (扣繳稅率為 20%)。

2). 過帳供應商發票

檢視模擬產生的財務輸入項：

3). 供應商發票付款

模擬付款文件，您可在下列數字中查看付款點產生扣繳稅明細項目。

針對台灣的扣繳稅過帳點，客戶目前可根據需求參考對應方案。

 

  • 扣繳稅報表

針對部份國家/地區，扣繳稅報表會根據自供應商和客戶文件收集的資訊產生，例如結清發票、供應商貸項通知單、訂金和結清的貸項通知單。系統會產生可提交給政府機關的內文檔案。部份國家/地區的 Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) 功能啟用後，您可使用 [執行法定報表] 產生扣繳稅報表。

例如，美國的扣繳稅報表如下：

美國扣繳稅 (1099-MISC) 報表

比利時的扣繳稅申報資訊如下：

比利時扣繳稅報表

然而，SAP 尚未提供台灣的標準扣繳稅申報報表，且可使用下列 CDS 檢視以取得對應扣繳稅資訊：

  • I_SupplierWithHoldingTax：供應商扣繳稅
  • I_CustomerWithTax：客戶扣繳稅
  • I_WithholdingTaxCode：扣繳稅碼
  • I_SuplrInvcHeaderWhldgTaxAPI01: 供應商發票上的扣繳稅

現在台灣客戶需要將扣繳稅申報報表傳送給SAP ERP公有雲產品部門，並期待未來台灣本土化功能中的扣繳稅申報報表呈現。如需客戶請求，請參閱下列詳細連結：

改善請求明細 - 客戶影響

從 CE2502 開始，SAP ERP公有雲提供新的應用程式「扣繳稅分析」，可回答問題「要如何計算此交易的扣繳稅？」下列是德國公司代碼的範例：

如需有關應用程式的資訊，請參閱輔助說明文件： 扣繳稅計算分析 | SAP Help Portal

然而，此應用程式暫時不支援台灣使用，提示下列資訊：

希望未來將開放該應用程式在台灣使用。

 

  • 結論

在台灣地區，扣繳稅適用於不同的收入類型，例如台灣內部產生的股利、租金、利息和權利金。要確保及時納稅，簡化稅收管理，落實各項合規制度。

在每月第十天前，扣繳稅申報人應當向財政部繳納稅款上個月，並在下一納稅年度1月前向稅務機關提交扣繳證明，具體規定上一年度繳納稅款和代扣稅款。如果1月連續有國定假日，則提交扣繳憑證的期間應延長至2月5日。對於向非居民或者外國企業繳納的在中華民國境內沒有常設機構的收入，扣繳代理人應當在代扣稅款後向財政部繳納代扣稅款，並在代扣後十日內向稅務機關提交扣繳稅款申報和收據。參考連結 [4]

像台灣很多稅種一樣，對報稅的誤解和錯過期限，可能會給台灣境內外經營的外商造成經濟損失。

 

  • 參考資訊
  • Help Portal 紀錄文件 | 扣繳稅
  •  虛擬資料模型和 CDS 檢視 | 供應商發票扣繳稅
  • 台灣扣繳稅指南 | 稅 | SongjerCPA
  • 持有代理人何時應退回並支付扣繳稅？
  • 美國扣繳稅 (1099-MISC) 報表
  • 比利時扣繳稅報表

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Taiwan Localization - Workaround Solution for Withholding Tax
entry.content:
Introduction: Since the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition standard does not provide withholding tax functionality localized for Taiwan, this blog is based mainly on references from other countries, and readers are welcome to make additional comments and suggestions.

Catalog:

  • Withholding Tax Introduction
  • Scenario for Posting Withholding Tax
  • Configure Withholding Tax in the System
  • Withholding Tax Demos
  • Report for Withholding Tax
  • Conclusion
  • Reference Information

______________________________________________________________

 

  • Withholding Tax Introduction

Withholding Tax deduction is dictated by the statutory requirements of the applicable country and hence it is purely a localization topic. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition it is addressed through the scope item 1J2- Advance Compliance Reporting.

Withholding tax is the tax that is charged when the payment flow starts in some countries/regions. Generally, the taxable party does not pay withholding tax directly to the tax authority. When a customer authorized to deduct withholding tax pays a vendor invoice that is subject to withholding tax, the customer subtracts the withholding tax amount from the payment amount. The customer then pays the withholding tax directly to the tax authority. However, for self-withholding, suppliers subject to taxes have the right to pay taxes directly to the authorities in this case.

In Taiwan, withholding tax is the amount of resident or non-resident income payments (payers) withheld by your company. If you are paying a non-local (foreign) supplier, you need to withhold a certain percentage of the invoice amount and pay the tax form to the tax office, as well as the balance to be paid to the foreign supplier.

The following payments are covered:

  • Dividends
  • Interest
  • Rental Income
  • Royalties
  • Payments for technical fees, services
  • Wages and Salaries
  • Commissions

Taiwan's withholding tax system applies to the various income earned by residents and non-residents, determining tax rates and regulations by income type and residence status of taxpayers. Different withholding tax rates apply for each type of payment and if there is a double tax agreement between Taiwan and a country in which a non-resident (foreign party) is a tax resident, the reduced tax rate may apply. The following table details the relevant forms for the different payments in the Taiwan withholding tax system.

Reference link [3]

Withholding tax example: Taiwan companies purchase professional services from foreign companies with an amount of $5,000 before tax, foreign companies will bear withholding tax, so Taiwan companies only need to pay US$4,000 to foreign companies, and the deduction of 1,000 US dollars (20% of $5,000 USD) should be paid to the tax authorities.

  • Scenario for Posting Withholding Tax
  1. Depending on the legal requirements of each country/region, withholding tax is calculated at different stages and posted to the corresponding withholding tax accounts. As a rule, withholding tax is posted at the same time as the payment is posted. In other words, the payment (payable) or incoming payment (receivable) is reduced by the withholding tax amount.
  2. In accounts payable, the supplier is excisable and the company is obliged to deduct withholding tax and pay it to the tax authorities on behalf of the supplier. In accounts receivable, the company itself is subject to tax. Customers who do business with this company deduct withholding tax and pay them to the tax authorities on behalf of the company. In both cases, the business partner of the taxable person/entity deducts the tax and pays it to the tax authority.
  3. In some countries/regions, such as Brazil, the Philippines, and Spain, withholding tax can or must be posted when the invoice is posted. This means that the receivable or payable amount is reduced by the withholding tax amount.
  4. In some countries/regions, such as Argentina and Italy, some business transactions require other types of postings. Withholding tax is posted as an offsetting entry to the expense account instead of the payment amount reduced by the withholding tax amount. You make the relevant settings for this when maintaining withholding tax codes.
  5. When resetting cleared items, the clearing document is automatically reversed if it contains withholding tax data. For withholding tax types with accumulation, the accumulated base amount is adjusted if the payment document is reversed.
  6. In this blog, the two scenarios of posting withholding tax at point of payment and posting withholding tax at point of payment are shown in the examples for Account Payable.

 

  • Configure Withholding Tax in the System

1. Activate Withholding Tax - 104228

To use withholding tax in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition (also known as extended withholding tax), you must first activate it.

2. Define Withholding Tax Keys – 106576

Withholding tax codes are used to identify different withholding tax types.

3. Define Withholding Tax Type for Payment Posting – 104226

Withholding taxes can be deducted at two points in time; they can be deducted at the time of invoicing or at payment. Therefore, for this withholding tax type, one withholding tax type is created for payments and one for invoices.

4. Define Withholding Tax Type for Invoice Posting – 104225

Withholding taxes can be deducted at two points in time; they can be deducted at the time of invoicing or at payment. Therefore, for this withholding tax type, one withholding tax type is created for payments and one for invoices.

5. Assign Withholding Tax Types to Company Code – 103914

6. Define Withholding Tax Codes - 104227

In the withholding tax codes, maintain different tax rates and maintain the basis for the corresponding deductions.

7. Define Min/Max Amounts for Withholding Codes– 103915

If required, you can define a minimum/maximum limit amount for which the withholding tax code is valid, or, if no amount is specified, there is no limit to the lower and lower amount for withholding tax.

8. Assign Withholding Tax key to Withholding Tax Code – 105553

9. Assign Exchange Rate Type for Withholding Tax Type - 104400

10. Automatic Account Determination for Withholding Tax Code– 100297

11. Check Withholding Tax Configuration

①Check Company Code

②Check Business Partner

③Check Withholding Tax Type

④Simulate Calculation

 

  • Withholding Tax Demos

Withholding tax in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is also called extended withholding tax and contains withholding tax types and withholding tax codes. The withholding tax type represents the base calculation rule. The withholding tax code represents a specific feature of these rules, especially the percentage rate.

If more than one withholding tax is required for a particular transaction, this is covered in the SAP system by defining several withholding tax types. When you enter line items, you can enter withholding tax data for each withholding tax type. Note, however, that if you have two or more sets of withholding tax codes and types in an invoice, you must decide whether all the proposed withholding tax codes and types are relevant for the transaction. If you do not calculate and post some of the proposed withholding tax, you must remove the transaction-independent withholding tax code.

1. Scenario: Post Withholding Tax at Point of Invoice

1). Maintain Business Partner Master Data

From the screenshot above, you can see that the vendor withholding tax type is set as the point in time of invoice posting to generate withholding tax line items with withholding tax code T1 that is 20% withholding tax rate.

2). Post Supplier Invoice

View the financial entries generated by the simulation:

You will see that a withholding tax document entry was generated to deduct the payable amount.

2. Scenario: Post Withholding Tax at Point of Payment

1). Maintain Business Partner Master Data

From the screenshot above, you can see that the vendor withholding tax type is set as the point of payment to generate withholding tax line items with withholding tax code T1, which is 20% withholding tax rate.

2). Post Supplier Invoice

View the financial entries generated by the simulation:

3). Payment of Supplier Invoice

Simulate the payment document and you can see that at the time of payment generates the withholding tax line items in the following figure.

Currently, for the withholding tax posting point in Taiwan, customers can choose the corresponding scenario as per their requirements.

 

  • Report for Withholding Tax

For some countries/regions, the withholding tax report is generated based on the information collected from supplier and customer documents, such as cleared invoices, supplier credit memos, down payments, and cleared credit memos. The system generates a text file that can be submitted to the government authorities. Once the Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) feature is active for some countries/regions, you can use the Run Statutory Reports to generate withholding tax report.

For example, the withholding tax report for the United States is as follows:

Withholding Tax (1099-MISC) Report for the US

The withholding tax reporting information for Belgium is as follows:

Withholding Tax Report in Belgium

However, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not yet provide a standard withholding tax declaration report for Taiwan and user can use the following CDS View to get the corresponding withholding tax information:

  • I_SupplierWithHoldingTax: Supplier Withholding Tax
  • I_CustomerWithTax: Customer Withholding Tax
  • I_WithholdingTaxCode: withholding tax code
  • I_SuplrInvcHeaderWhldgTaxAPI01 : Withholding Tax on Supplier Invoice

Customers already sent requirement of withholding tax declaration reports to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition product departments and look forward to the withholding tax declaration report delivered by SAP in the future localization functionality for Taiwan. Please refer to the following link to check detail request on customer influence portal:

Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence

Starting with CE2502, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition offers a new application, Withholding Tax Calculation Analysis, which answers the question, “How do you calculate withholding tax for this transaction?”, as follows is an example of a German company code:

For information about the app, refer to the help document: Withholding Tax Calculation Analysis | SAP Help Portal

However, the app is currently not supported for Taiwan and prompts the following information:

Hope the app will be open for Taiwan in the future.

 

  • Conclusion

In the Taiwan region, withholding tax is applicable for different income types, such as dividends, rents, interest, and royalties generated internally in Taiwan. It is important to ensure timely taxation, simplify tax management, and enforce compliance.

The withholding agent shall, prior to the tenth day of each month, pay to the Treasury the taxes withheld the previous month and make out withholding certificates and submit them to the tax offices not later than the January of the following tax year, specifying the payment made and the tax withheld in the previous year. In the case that three national holidays occur in immediate succession in January the period for the submission of the withholding certificates shall be extended to February 5 .For income paid to non-residents or foreign businesses having no permanent establishment in the ROC, the withholding agent shall pay to the Treasury the taxes withheld and file a withholding report and the receipts with the tax offices within ten days after withholding.

Reference link [4]

Like many tax types in Taiwan, misunderstandings of tax declarations or missed deadlines can cause economic losses for foreign companies inside and outside Taiwan.

 

  • Reference Information
  1. Help Portal Documentation | Withholding Tax
  2. Virtual Data Model and CDS Views | Withholding Tax for Supplier Invoice
  3. Taiwan withholding Tax Guide | Tax | SongjerCPA
  4. When shall a holding agent file returns and make payments of tax withheld?
  5. Withholding Tax (1099-MISC) Report in the US
  6. Withholding Tax Report in Belgium

entry.title: Simplifying SAP Note Implementation with TCI for Electronic Documents
entry.content:
We're excited to announce the release of Transport-Based Correction Instructions (TCI) for electronic document processing, a game-changer for implementing SAP Notes. TCI streamlines the process, making it faster and more efficient.

What is TCI?

Transport-Based Correction Instructions (TCI) are pre-packaged solutions that bundle multiple SAP Notes into a single transport request. This approach eliminates the need for manual SAP Note implementation, reducing complexity and saving time.

New TCIs available for electronic documents

Electronic communication integration framework

SAP Note 3559476 - Transport based Correction Instructions (TCI) for the Electronic Communication Integration Framework

  • This TCI includes all SAP Notes published before December 31, 2024, for packages GLO_GEN_GCO_COMMUNICATION and GLO_GEN_GCO_COMMUNICATION_AIF.
  • Application component: CA-GTF-CSC-DRC-COM 
  • Supported releases:
    • S4CORE: Versions 104 to 108
    • SAP_APPL: Version 606 (SP12), 617 (SP05), and 618 (initial support package)

Electronic document processing framework

SAP Note 3545430 - Transport-Based Correction Instructions (TCI) for the Electronic Document Processing Framework

  • This TCI includes the following:
    • Framework SAP Notes up to Framework 36
    • Inbound Message Handler
    • Customer Invoice (Peppol and via E-Mail) core components
    • Supplier Invoice (Peppol and via E-Mail) core components
    • Peppol Customer Invoice (Austria, Belgium, Germany, Denmark, Netherlands, Norway, Sweden, Ireland, Poland)
    • Peppol Supplier Invoice (Received by E-Mail) for Germany
  • Supported releases:
    • S4CORE: Versions 104 to 108
    • SAP_APPL: Version 606 (SP12), 617 (SP05), and 618 (initial support package)

Why TCI matters

TCI implementation simplifies the process, ensuring that your systems are up-to-date with the latest SAP Notes without the hassle of manual updates.

Give us your feedback

To ensure that we’re covering what you need, please leave your feedback at the bottom of this blog post!

#SAPGoGlobal #SAPLocalization

entry.title: 集合貼：台灣地區常見本地化需求在SAP ERP公有雲中的實施方式建議
entry.content:
SAP ERP公有雲解決方案針對台灣地區提供了許多本地化功能，例如符合當地法規的財務報表、稅務申報、電子發票管理、資料合規處理等，完整信息可參考 Localization for Taiwan in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition。

過去幾年來，許多用戶和實施商在具體應用該系統後給予SAP大量有益的反饋。本文將聚焦其中幾個重要話題，借助架構師的見解，為台灣地區的SAP應用生態圈的客戶和實施夥伴提供參考，幫助企業更好地利用SAP ERP雲系統來推動數位化管理。

 

基於利潤中心的成本核算

利潤中心評價是企業財務管理中不可或缺的一部分，專注於評估各業務單位或部門的財務表現。透過將收入和成本歸屬於特定的利潤中心，企業能夠分析盈利能力、促進問責制及支持戰略決策制定。目前，SAP ERP公有雲暫不支援此類業務需求，参考：3371144 - Profit Center Valuation is not supported in SAP S/4HANA Cloud system 、Profit Center Valuation | SAP Customer Influence Portal 。建議企業在前期溝通中了解產品現狀及設計理念，並可能考慮依諮詢商的業務優化建議調整管理方式，運用SAP提供的最佳實踐。

 

外勞採購的稅代扣代繳與申報

在台灣，透過外勞的採購來支持企業內外需求相當常見。當地法規要求企業為這類採購進行稅代扣代繳，具體可參考：Comprehensive Overview of Withholding Tax in Taiwan。目前，SAP ERP公有雲尚未提供全面的本地化解決方案，但可了解架構師提供的變通應用方案：SAP ERP公有雲台灣本土化 - 扣繳稅方案建議。

若貴司或您的企業客戶有類似需求，歡迎透過企業S-User帳號登入以下站點頁面，投下您寶貴的一票，以助SAP產品部門更好地規劃相關功能的實現：Withholding Tax Request | SAP Customer Influence Portal。

 

台灣統一發票管理及VAT申報

依台灣地區相關法規，目前（2025年）有效的統一發票形式共有五種。SAP ERP公有雲提供了電子發票的標準端到端解決方案。關於統一發票在SAP ERP公有雲中的管理建議、方案應用注意事項及與加值中心集成申報稅務的實施方式建議，請參閱：統一發票在 SAP ERP 公有雲系統中的實施方案建議。

 

LCM報表

LCM報表是依IFRS準則下LCNRV（Lower Cost or Net Realizable Value，成本與可變現淨值孰低法，參考IAS 2 Inventories）庫存評價方法的財務報表。根據台灣法規，已上市或準備IPO的企業需要定期披露LCM報表。目前，SAP ERP公有雲中的庫存評價標準APP尚未支援LCNRV方法，企業需客製化報表以滿足相關法規需求。如需此功能，請在以下頁面投票：Inventory Valuation Enhancement for IFRS Compliance with LCNRV Method | SAP Customer Influence Portal。

 

庫齡表

台灣上市企業需藉ERP系統的庫齡表有效管理庫存資產，確保合規及財務報表的準確性，改善資產流動性，減少損失，提高競爭力。SAP ERP公有雲目前提供標準應用 "Inventory KPI Analysis" 供企業分析庫存指標。企業或SAP實施夥伴亦可透過客製化報表滿足深度定製庫齡表的需求。更多相關博客可在 SAP Community 查詢。

entry.title: 集锦帖：台湾地区常见本地化需求在SAP ERP公有云中的实施方式建议
entry.content:
SAP ERP公有云解决方案针对台湾地区提供了许多本地化功能，例如符合当地法规的财务报表、税务申报、电子发票管理、数据合规处理等，完整信息可参考 S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition中针对台湾的本地化功能。

过去几年中，许多用户和实施商在应用该系统后给予SAP大量有益反馈。本文将聚焦其中几个重要话题，通过架构师的见解，为台湾地区的SAP应用生态圈的客户和实施伙伴提供参考，帮助企业更好地利用SAP ERP云系统推动数字化管理。

 

基于利润中心的成本核算

利润中心评估是企业财务管理中不可或缺的一部分，专注于评估各业务单位或部门的财务表现。通过将收入和成本归集到特定的利润中心，企业能够分析盈利能力、促进问责制及支持战略决策制定。目前，SAP ERP公有云暂不支持此类业务需求，参考：3371144 - SAP S/4HANA Cloud 系统中不支持利润中心评估、Profit Center Valuation | SAP Customer Influence Portal。建议企业在前期沟通中了解SAP产品现状及设计理念，并根据咨询商的业务优化建议调整企业管理方式，运用SAP提供的最佳实践。

 

外劳采购的税代扣代缴与申报

在中国台湾，通过外劳的采购来支持企业内外需求相当常见。当地法规要求企业为这类采购进行税代扣代缴，具体可参考：Comprehensive Overview of Withholding Tax in Taiwan。目前，SAP ERP公有云尚未提供全面的本地化解决方案，但可了解架构师提供的变通应用方案：SAP ERP公有云台湾本地化-代扣税实施建议。

若贵司或您的企业客户有类似需求，欢迎通过企业S-User账户登录以下页面，投下您宝贵的一票，以助SAP产品部门更好地规划相关功能的实现：Withholding Tax Request | SAP Customer Influence Portal。

 

台湾统一发票管理及VAT申报

根据台湾地区相关法规，目前（2025年）有效的统一发票形式共有五种。SAP ERP公有云提供了电子发票的标准端到端解决方案。关于统一发票在SAP ERP公有云中的管理建议、方案应用注意事项、及与加值中心集成申报VAT的实施方式建议，请参阅：台湾地区统一发票在 SAP ERP 公有云系统中的实施方案建议。

 

LCM报表

LCM报表是依据IFRS准则下LCNRV（成本与可变现净值孰低法，参考 IAS 2 Inventories ）库存估值方法的财务报表。根据台湾法规，已上市或准备IPO的企业需要定期披露LCM报表。目前，SAP ERP公有云中的库存估值标准APP尚未支持LCNRV方法，企业需定制报表以满足相关法规需求。如需此功能，请在以下页面投票：Inventory Valuation Enhancement for IFRS Compliance with LCNRV Method | SAP Customer Influence Portal。

 

库龄表

台湾上市企业需通过ERP系统的库龄表有效管理库存资产，确保合规及财务报表的准确性，改善资产流动性，减少损失，提高竞争力。SAP ERP公有云目前提供标准应用 "库存KPI分析" 供企业分析库存指标。企业或SAP实施伙伴亦可通过定制化报表满足深度定制库龄表的需求。更多相关博客可在 SAP Community 查询。

entry.title: Recommendations for Implementing Common Localization Requirements for Taiwan with SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition offers various localized features tailored for the Taiwan region, such as financial reporting compliant with local regulations, tax reporting, electronic invoice management, and data compliance processing. For complete information, please refer to Localization for Taiwan in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Over the past few years, many users and implementation partners have provided SAP with valuable feedback following their practical application of the system. This blog focuses on several important topics, drawing insights from SAP solution architects to offer guidance to clients and implementation partners within Taiwan's SAP ecosystem. The goal is to help businesses better utilize the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system to drive digital management.

 

Profit Center Valuation

Profit center valuation is an important part of corporate financial management in some enterprises, focusing on evaluating the financial performance of various business units or departments. By attributing revenue and costs to specific profit centers, businesses can analyze profitability, enhance accountability, and support strategic decision-making. Currently, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not support this business requirement. Refer to: 3371144 - Profit Center Valuation is not supported in SAP S/4HANA Cloud system and Profit Center Valuation | SAP Customer Influence Portal. It's recommended for enterprises to understand the current SAP product status and design philosophy during preliminary discussions in the Discover phase and consider adjusting management approaches based on business optimization suggestions from consultants, using SAP's best practices.

 

Withholding and Reporting Tax for Foreign Labor Procurement

In Taiwan, it is common to procure foreign labor to support internal operation and customer sales business needs. Local regulations in Taiwan require companies to withhold and report taxes for such procurements. For details, refer to: Comprehensive Overview of Withholding Tax in Taiwan. Currently, the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not offer a comprehensive localized solution specifically for Taiwan, but alternative application solutions provided by architects can be explored: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Taiwan Localization - Workaround Solution for Withholding Tax.

If your company or corporate client has similar needs, feel free to log in with your company's S-User account to the following webpage to cast your valuable vote, thereby aiding the SAP product department in better planning the implementation of related functionalities: Withholding Tax Request | SAP Customer Influence Portal.

 

Government Uniform Invoice (GUI) Management and VAT Reporting in Taiwan

According to Taiwan's relevant regulations, there are currently (as of 2025) five valid forms of GUI. The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides a standard end-to-end solution for electronic invoicing. For recommendations on managing GUI within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, application considerations, and implementation suggestions for tax reporting integration with value-added centers, please refer to: Expert Insights on Adopting Solutions for Taiwan's Government Uniform Invoices (GUI).

 

LCM Reporting

LCM reports are financial statements based on the IFRS-standard LCNRV (Lower Cost or Net Realizable Value, according to IAS 2 Inventories) inventory valuation method. In Taiwan, companies listed or preparing for an IPO are required to regularly disclose LCM reports. Currently, the inventory valuation standard app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not support the LCNRV method, requiring companies to customize reports to fulfill related regulatory requirements. If you need this functionality, please vote on the following page: Inventory Valuation Enhancement for IFRS Compliance with LCNRV Method | SAP Customer Influence Portal.

 

Inventory Aging Report

Taiwan-listed companies need to effectively manage inventory assets using Inventory Aging Reports within ERP systems to ensure compliance and accuracy of financial statements, improve asset liquidity, reduce losses, and enhance competitiveness. The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition currently offers a standard application "Inventory KPI Analysis" for enterprises to analyze inventory metrics. Enterprises or SAP implementation partners can also fulfill the need for deeply customized Inventory Aging Reports through custom reporting. More related blogs can be found at  SAP Community .

entry.title: Explore Netherlands EPR Declaration for 2024 with SAP Responsible Design and Production
entry.content:
We are thrilled to introduce a new report category in SAP Responsible Design and Production to support the mandate for Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) reporting for Netherlands in 2024. This update ensures compliance with the latest regulatory requirements announced by Verpact.

 

SAP’s Circular Economy Vision

SAP is committed to transforming businesses and value chains for a circular economy, aiming for a future with zero waste. Our technology focuses on:

  • Eliminating waste and pollution
  • Circulating materials and keeping products in use
  • Regenerating natural systems

For more information, see Circular Economy Solutions. 

 

SAP Responsible Design and Production

SAP Responsible Design and Production provides intelligence that allows your company to monitor, measure, and act, so you can eliminate waste and circulate materials. SAP Responsible Design and Production calculates EPR obligations, plastic taxes, and corporate commitments to optimize material choices, reduce fees, and reduce risks coming from unforeseen regulatory costs and non-compliance. It enables you to make effective decisions with strategic data insights and analytics. 

For more information on the product or to request a demo, access SAP Responsible Design and Production.

 

Key Updates in the 2024 Netherlands EPR Regulation

The Netherlands has introduced significant changes to its EPR reporting requirements for 2024, affecting packaging producers and importers. Key changes include the following:

  • Updated Reporting Structure: The structure and format of EPR reports have been modified to align with the new regulatory framework, enabling you to keep up with the changing EU legislations.
  • New Packaging Categories: Additional types of packaging have been introduced as relevant for reporting, such as reusable cups and plastic bottles without deposit.
  • Enhanced Single-Use Plastic (SUP) Determination Logic: A revised methodology to identify and categorize single-use plastics for more accurate reporting.
  • Fee Modulation: New logic for discounts based on packaging material and recyclability.
  • Changes to EPR Fees for Packaging: Modifications affecting cost calculations for producers.

 

How SAP Responsible Design and Production Supports Compliance

Our new report category offers the following:

  • Automated Compliance Reporting: Generates reports adhering to the new mandate.
  • Customizable Data Inputs: Adapts reporting parameters based on business-specific packaging data.
  • Accurate Fee Calculation: Applies the latest EPR fee structures and modulations.
  • Enhanced Data Validation: Minimizes errors with built-in validation mechanisms.

 

For more details on this update and implementation, see Change in tariffs for packaging data reporting in 2024 in the Regulatory Change Manager, refer to our official documentation, or contact your account executive.

 

 

There are many features and functions which have been released in SAP Responsible Design and Production. You can check the What’s New section in the SAP Help Portal to know the latest updates for every new release.

For an outlook on upcoming regulatory changes and features, you can consult the Regulatory Change Manager and the SAP Road Map Explorer for SAP Responsible Design and Production. 

Stay up to date with EPR mandates and plastic taxes with SAP Responsible Design and Production!

entry.title: SAP Document Reporting Compliance in Latvia, Estonia, and Lithuania
entry.content:
As businesses in Latvia, Estonia, and Lithuania adapt to the evolving digital economy, electronic invoicing has become a key component of streamlining operations and ensuring compliance with local tax regulations. SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) Baltics solution offers businesses in the Baltics an efficient way to manage their invoicing processes, enhance accuracy, and stay compliant with each country’s electronic invoicing requirements.

Latvia

Regulatory Change Manager : Latvia 

As of Jan 1st, 2026, Latvia will implement mandatory electronic invoicing for B2B. This requirement extends to all business registered in Latvia.

Key Details:

  • PEPPOL BIS 3.0 Format
  • Additional technical specifications will be released in May 2025
  • Alongside e-Invoices business will be required to submit e-Invoice data to SRS (State Revenue Service)
  • B2G Contracts there is a transition period

Our Crimson DRC Baltics Solution is already live for B2G clients in Latvia

Estonia:

Regulatory Change Manager : Estonia 

Estonia has been progressively advancing its e-Invoicing rollout, as of July 1st 2025 business can insist on receiving e-Invoices from suppliers in UBL format, though the old Estonian format is also in use.

By Jan 1st 2027 it is expected all invoices that subject to VAT must be e-Invoices.

As of July 1st 2025 B2G customers will begin to mandate e-Invoicing

Lithuania

Regulatory Change Manager : Lithuania 

In Lithuania e-Invoicing for B2G has been in use for a number of years with the move to the SABIS platform in 2024 further building on the original eSaskaita

On Jan 1st 2025 much of the B2G invoice market was mandated to be sent via SABIS but currently B2B is optional however we are now seeing many organisation’s already preparing for mandatory e-Invoicing.

We believe in 2026 e-Invoicing for B2B will become mandatory aligning with the broader movements in the EU and the Baltic states in general.

Key Details:

  • SABIS Platform
  • PEPPOL Integration
  • Digital Signature is not mandatory but can be used
  • Archiving for 10 years is legally required

Our CrimsonSAP DRC solution is already live for B2G & optional B2B where agreed and thus we can help prepare you for the mandatory phase by getting you ready early.

What is SAP DRC?

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC)Baltics solution is designed to help organizations comply with electronic invoicing regulations and meet tax reporting requirements. In the Baltics, where e-invoicing is gaining traction, this solution ensures seamless integration with tax authorities, such as Latvia’s SRS (State Revenue Service), Estonia’s E-File, and Lithuania’s VMI (State Tax Inspectorate).

Each of the three Baltic countries has its own set of requirements for electronic invoicing. However, they share the common goal of reducing tax fraud, improving efficiency, and enhancing transparency in business transactions.

Benefits of Using SAP DRC for E-Invoicing

  1. Simplified Compliance: SAP DRC automatically formats and sends invoices to the respective tax authorities in real time, ensuring that companies comply with local tax regulations without manual intervention.
  2. Cost Efficiency: By automating the invoicing process, businesses save time and reduce the costs associated with manual processing and errors.
  3. Improved Accuracy: SAP DRC ensures that invoices are created in the correct format, minimizing the chances of non-compliance and errors that could lead to penalties.
  4. Data Security: With its secure cloud-based solution, SAP DRC guarantees the safety of sensitive business data, making it easier for companies to maintain a reliable and audit-friendly invoicing process.

The adoption of electronic invoicing in Latvia, Estonia, and Lithuania is part of the broader effort to digitize business processes and simplify compliance with tax regulations. SAP DRC provides businesses in these countries with the tools they need to streamline their invoicing processes, reduce administrative costs, and meet local regulatory standards. With e-invoicing becoming increasingly important, companies that embrace solutions like SAP DRC can stay ahead of the curve and focus on their core operations.

How can CrimsonSAP help business to achieve compliance?

Through our team in Lviv and Estonia we are already actively working with the Tax Authorities across the Baltics, our solution is already live across all three countries for Customers and Suppliers as well the ability to send & receive from the wider PEPPOL network.

As part of our work with SAP to build the POC on Document Reporting Compliance Cloud (DRCCe) Extensibility our solution works seamlessly on both DRCCe & Integration Suite.

Our access point in the Baltics is already registered and utilized across the Baltics.

We provide an E2E DRC Solution for AR / AP & SAFT Reporting.

Using standard AIF Mapping across the Baltics and a single UBL Format we have been able to deploy our solution across multiple countries with 2nd and 3rd countries deployed in as little as 4-5 weeks.

Key Features:

  • Generation of electronic invoices in XML format (FI, SD) for B2B and B2G
  • Tax invoice
  • Correction invoices
  • Self-billing invoices
  • FICA and RE-FX billing
  • Manual or automated invoice submission
  • Conversion of xml invoices into pdf readable format
  • Receiving of e-invoices in XML format
  • XMLs can be integrated to VIM or via API to other Vendor software
  • HTML view of the xml

Estonia Demo S/42023 

Solution on SAP Store Shortly

entry.title: Navigating the Global Landscape with Cloud ERP: Insights from PwC's Journey
entry.content:
In a rapidly evolving business environment, organizations are constantly seeking to unlock more productivity, efficiency, and innovation. One proven catalyst for this progress is Enterprise Resource Planning or ERP systems run on public cloud infrastructures. A cloud-based ERP system not only helps to streamline operations and ensure seamless data flow across various business functions but also brings in scalability, reducing the strain on in-house IT infrastructures.

At the nexus of ERP technology, international business, and regulatory compliance, sits a critical concept: Localization. It’s an often-overlooked aspect that can transform the business' functionality globally.

Expanding Horizons: Unlocking Global Potential with Cloud ERP Localization

Most companies operate across multiple jurisdictions with diverse tax guidelines, legal frameworks, and statutory requirements. Ensuring compliance with local standards while maintaining a globally uniform ERP system can be a considerable challenge. That's where ERP localization comes in.

Localization refers to the adaptation of an ERP system to cater to the legislative, cultural, operational, and market requirements of a particular country or a region. As part of ERP implementation, localization accounts for languages, taxation rules, fiscal obligations, and various other national considerations.

Mr. Marc Hoessels, Managing Director of PwC, explains the profound impact of localization on cloud ERP systems. Over the past eight years, Mr. Hoessels’ primary role has been to ensure tax-and-legal compliance for their global clients. He has successfully embraced localization, leading to broader adoption and substantial benefits for their clients. Mr. Hoessels adds, "With 50,000 professionals worldwide in 150+ countries, implementing a centralized approach called an acceleration package helps us comply with local requirements across different jurisdictions smoothly."

Why Choose Cloud ERP?

Cloud-based ERP solutions offer numerous benefits that make them appealing to businesses across various sectors. According to IDC’s SAP Global Future of Trust & Security Survey, the top reasons for moving to a cloud ERP system include better security, compliance, privacy, and risk posture (45%), and better alignment with company values and ESG initiatives (34%). Furthermore, cloud-based ERP users report much greater trust in their systems compared to on-prem ERP users, with 31% of cloud-based ERP users reporting 100% trust in their ERP provider.

Mr. Michael Rasch, a partner at PwC's German practice and the leader of PwC’s Global SAP Alliance, provides further insight into why PwC chose SAP's cloud ERP. Mr. Rasch shares, "PwC is an SAP user itself. We are one of the largest clients of SAP when it comes to the public cloud edition. Before we moved to the S/4HANA journey with SAP, we did a calculation on how many different accounting systems we had around the globe. We figured out we had more than 50 different accounting systems, which really made no sense for a professional service firm. That's when we decided to adopt SAP's 'golden standard' for our finance-related data."

PwC started implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition in central European countries like Germany, Austria, Switzerland, Belgium, the Netherlands, and Turkey. According to Mr. Rasch, "We are now rolling it out to the U.S., Japan, and other large markets. Using the public cloud also helps us limit variances and ensures compliance with specific national laws, using tools like SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)."

The public cloud ERP's compatibility with functional localization facilitates adherence to laws, regulations, and standards in various jurisdictions, all while using a single, unified system. Therefore, alongside these benefits, organizations can harness additional productivity gains through ERP localization.

"Using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition helps us streamline our accounting systems across our 156 firms," Mr. Rasch explains. "It ensures that the larger entities in our network derive significant value from a standardized and efficient system."

These insights from the IDC’s SAP Global Future of Trust & Security Survey and Mr. Rasch's experience illustrate the significant advantages of cloud ERP solutions, including increased security, compliance, and trust in the systems used.

From Experience to Expertise

PwC leverages their own experience as a customer of cloud ERP to enhance the services they provide to their clients. This dual perspective allows them to understand the challenges and solutions intimately. PwC approaches localization with a clear understanding of a client's operations and the jurisdictions they are active in.

PwC provides a centralized approach to localization through regional expertise. Teams across Asia, the Middle East, Africa, Europe, and the Americas leverage their local knowledge of SAP solutions to aid clients' global transitions seamlessly. Mr. Hoessels notes, "The new SAP public cloud offerings, such as time-dependent tax code stamping, have proven very useful. We maintain these rate changes as a managed service, ensuring clients stay compliant with local tax regulations."

Summary

Embracing a public cloud-based ERP system, coupled with effective localization, can substantially bolster operational efficiency, regulatory compliance, and global scalability. The partnership between PwC and SAP exemplifies the power of combining advanced ERP solutions with deep localization to navigate the global business landscape effectively.

Mr.Rasch reiterates their growth and success in this partnership, having seen their practice grow around 20% year over year in the past three years, driven by the robust needs of clients and the compelling offerings from SAP. With this dynamic collaboration, PwC leads the way in leveraging cloud ERP and localization to drive transformative success for its clients globally.

Learn about Localization Today:

SAP Community for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Localization

Localization Capabilities on sap.com

SAP Community for Localization 

Building Better Trust in Business with Cloud ERP Investments

entry.title: GRC, Trade and Tax with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm thrilled to share an update regarding our latest release - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 for GRC, Trade, and Tax. This update highlights many significant innovations from SAP Risk and Assurance Management, SAP Watch List Screening, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, as well as SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance.

For a quick overview of the top release highlights for GRC with 2502, you can watch my video. If you're interested in a complete overview of all release highlights, scroll down to the table of contents.

 

 

This blog covers the following innovations:

SAP Risk and Assurance Management

  • Qualitative Risk Management Capabilities
  • Comprehensive Customer-Specific Reporting
  • Enabling Manual Procedures: Offline Execution

SAP Watch List Screening

  • Custom Watch List - Uploading of Custom Watch List Content
  • Unified SAP Fiori Apps and Process Optimization

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

  • Receiving Electronic Invoices From Suppliers By E-Mail (Germany)
  • Migration of Online Submission to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance

  • Extended Access Governance: Support for Additional Authorization in SAP BTP
  • Automatic Deprovisioning for Expired Business Role Assignments
  • Additional Risk Details in the Approver View Through a More Extensive Risk Perspective

 

SAP Risk and Assurance Management

Qualitative Risk Management Capabilities

The GRC Risk service offers a risk management solution with capabilities including risk documentation, risk analysis, and tracking of risk treatments. In addition to the existing quantitative risk analysis, it is now also possible to perform a qualitative risk analysis.

Value Proposition

  • Simple and quick - Enable users to assess risks without using precise numbers to determine impact or probability
  • Easy to perform and doesn‘t require expertise or training
  • Encourages collaboration and input from various stakeholders
  • Can be adapted to a wide range of industries and scenarios

Capabilities

  • Addition of qualitative risk assessment to the quantitative method
  • Qualitative risk assessment is a non-numerical approach to evaluating risks
  • Identify and prioritize risks based on their likelihood of occurring and the severity of their impact, using descriptive categories like low, medium, or high
  • Unified scoring to represent severity of risks regardless of quantitative or qualitative assessment approach to ensure comparability of assessment outcomes

 

Video 2: In addition to the existing quantitative risk analysis, it is now also possible to perform a qualitative risk analysis in SAP Risk and Assurance Management

For more information, see: Risk Analysis

Back to top

 

Comprehensive Customer-Specific Reporting

SAP Risk and Assurance Management supports comprehensive customer-specific reporting by making consolidated data available for reporting tools such as SAP Analytics Cloud.

This enables customers to analyze and visualize aspects like Number of Issues by Control over a Certain Time Period. 

Value Proposition

  • Analyze data related to different business objects to get deeper insights.
  • Optimized for reporting purposes.
  • Easy to use and simple to configure.

Capabilities

  • Consistent and consolidated data.
  • Provided via OData interface.
  • Consumable by third-party analytical solutions.

Fig. 1: SAP Risk and Assurance Management supports comprehensive customer-specific reporting

For more information, see: GRC Data Consolidation Service

Back to top

 

Enabling Manual Procedures: Offline Execution

Manual procedures can now be performed offline by procedure owners, meaning that they can be completed wherever they are needed. A template is available for download in the Offline Assigned Manual Procedures app which can then be distributed by compliance managers via email to all relevant people.

Value Proposition

  • Complete manual procedures anywhere, with or without an internet connection.
  • Aggregate data collected by several procedure owners in one place.
  • Leverage familiar tools like spreadsheets for greater ease of use.
  • Share templates with occasional business users in supported languages with minimal administration.

Capabilities 

  • Templates support fast and easy to use manual procedures to ensure that no time is lost.
  • Distribution of templates by compliance managers enables procedure owners to focus on the task at hand with minimal administrative effort.
  • Ability to perform manual procedures offline ensures maximum flexibility.
  • Enables compliance managers to have a better overview of collected data and ongoing tasks.

Fig. 2: In SAP Risk and Assurance Management, manual procedures can now be performed offline by procedure owners

For more information, see: Perform Offline-Assigned Manual Procedures

Back to top

 

SAP Watch List Screening

Custom Watch List - Uploading of Custom Watch List Content

If you wish to use a custom watch list for screening, instead of or additionally to the watch list provided by SAP, you can now use the Watch List API.
To select the watch lists that should be used for screening, you can use the new app Configure Watch List Screening.

Value Proposition

  • Use a custom watch list for screening

Capabilities

  • Replace the provided watch list or use the custom watch list as supplement

Video 3: With SAP Watch List Screening, it is now possible to use custom watch lists in addition to or as a replacement

For more information, see the Administration Guide:  Watch List API (optional)

Back to top

 

Unified SAP Fiori Apps and Process Optimization

You can now use a new app for ad hoc screening, a new app for reviewing screening hits and a new app for managing automatic screening decisions. The new apps have been designed to enhance your experience with more robust features, a streamlined interface and context-sensitive help. The new apps will eventually replace the old apps.

Value Proposition

  • More robust features
  • Streamlined interface

Capabilities 

  • Context-sensitive help

Fig. 3: SAP Watch List Screening now offers three new apps: one for ad hoc screening, one for reviewing screening hits and one for managing automatic screening decisions.

For more information, see: 

  • Ad Hoc Screening
  • Review Screening Hits
  • Manage Automatic Screening Decisions

Back to top

 

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

Receiving Electronic Invoices From Suppliers By E-Mail (Germany)

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance offers a process to validate electronic documents sent to you by your suppliers by e-mail to ensure they meet the European standard EN16931. You can use this process to comply with the new German regulation to be technically capable of receiving electronic invoices as of January 1, 2025.

When you receive electronic invoices or credit notes from your supplier by e-mail, you can upload the extracted electronic invoices (XML file) and create electronic documents for them in the Manage Electronic Documents app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. You can then validate them and display them in HTML format using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.

In addition, you can use SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management to automatically extract and upload the XML from the received email. The validation in the cloud edition can be automated as well as the posting of the supplier invoice for further processing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Value Proposition

  • Ensure compliance with the new German regulation to be able to receive invoices from suppliers electronically.
  • Ensure compliance with the German electronic invoicing standards:
  • XRechnung standard using the Cross Industry Invoice (UN/CEFACT) syntax.
  • XRechnung standard using the UBL Invoice or UBL Credit Note syntax.

Capabilities

  • Upload supplier invoices extracted from emails you receive from your supplier to the Manage Electronic Documents app
  • Creation of electronic documents for the supplier invoices in the Manage Electronic Documents app
  • Validation of electronic documents using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition
  • Display of supplier invoices in HTML format
  • Integration with incoming automation solution such as SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management

Fig. 4: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance allows you to validate electronic documents sent to you by your suppliers by e-mail to ensure they meet the European standard EN16931

For more information, see: Electronic Supplier Invoices (Receipt by E-Mail)

Back to top

 

Migration of Online Submission to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

In some countries/regions, you can submit your statutory reports electronically by using the web services provided by the respective government agencies. The end-to-end process of submitting statutory reports electronically to the authorities requires that you integrate your system with the service that actually transmits the statutory report electronically. This direct submission feature was supported via SAP Document and Reporting Compliance service and is now migrated to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.

Value Proposition

  • One single channel for all submissions across countries and processes
  • Simplify IT landscape and reduce total cost of ownership
  • Manage your integrations to tax authorities within your global SAP BTP account

Capabilities 

  • Centralized hub for all submissions to authorities, including reports and other requirements (e.g. HCM)
  • Standardized integration between SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition introduced with scope item 78L. One connection across countries and scenarios

Fig. 5: The direct electronic submission feature for statutory reports has been migrated to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

For more information, see: Submitting Statutory Reports Using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition

Back to top

 

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance

Extended Access Governance: Support for Additional Authorization in SAP BTP

The SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance application enables comprehensive risk assessment of permission-level authorization data by extracting BTP roles and scopes, while also providing the capability to define rules.

Value Proposition

  • Empowers organizations to enhance security and compliance by enabling precise risk assessments at the permission level
  • Improved operational efficiency, reduced risk exposure, and strengthened trust in enterprise access management processes

Capabilities 

  • Define rules at BTP roles as action and scopes as permissions
  • Execute risk assessment at granular permission-level authorization data

Fig. 5: SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance enables comprehensive risk assessment of permission-level authorization data by extracting BTP roles and scopes

For more information, see: SAP Busines Technology Platform - Cloud Foundry | SAP Help Portal

Back to top

 

Automatic Deprovisioning for Expired Business Role Assignments

Implementing an efficient process to automatically deprovision expired roles eliminates the need for manual review and identification of expired assignments. This automation ensures that relevant audit information is consistently updated to accurately reflect changes in role assignments, supporting compliance and streamlined access management.

Value Proposition

  • Streamline the Deprovisioning Process: Utilize the "Deprovision Expired Business Role Assignments" job to automate the identification and removal of expired business role assignments, eliminating the need for manual review.
  • Enhance Access Control: Ensure that users are promptly removed from roles they no longer need or are unauthorized to access, helping maintain strict access compliance.
  • Maintain Accurate Audit Trails: Automatically update audit logs and the "Business Role User Assigned" tab to reflect the deprovisioning of roles, ensuring a clear record of changes for compliance and review purposes.

Capabilities 

  • Administrators can now automate the removal of expired business role assignments by scheduling the "Deprovision Expired Business Role Assignments" job through the Job Scheduler app.

Fig. 7: SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance enables automatic deprovisioning for expired business role assignments

For more information, see: Help Document: Deprovision Expired Business Role Assignments

Back to top

 

Additional Risk Details in the Approver View Through a More Extensive Risk Perspective

An improved inbox view for approvers in the Access Request app, offering a more extensive risk perspective with additional details on risk factors, functions, actions, and authorization objects related to access.

Value Proposition

  • Equip approvers with comprehensive insights into risks, actions, and permissions that contribute to elevated access risks for users.

Capabilities 

  • More-extensive risk information, including additional information concerning risk factors, functions, actions, and authorization objects associated with access for the Access Approver view

Fig. 8: In SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, the inbox view for approvers in the Access Request app has been improved

For more information, see: Help Document: Access Request - Detailed Risk View

Back to top

 

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and X.

 

 

More innovation can be found in the What's new viewer

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

 

 

The Future of Business, Today: Get Your Access to AI-Assisted Features Thanks to this Limited Promotion Now! 

The 2502 update is a strategic investment that combines advanced AI with powerful enterprise management tools. Whether scaling your business, enhancing productivity, or building sustainable operations, its capabilities help support a more agile and resilient future.

Sign up for following features through the limited Promotion for AI-assisted Features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (for all countries, customers, and partners):

  • The easy filter and smart summarization features in SAP Fiori allow for efficient, natural language filtering of business data and seamless summarization capabilities, enabling users to quickly extract relevant information and jump-start subsequent collaboration. 
  • Facilitate easier understanding and interpretation of financial data with insights from cost center reviews and explanations of fixed-asset depreciation keys.

 

 

Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!   

Between January 21 and January 24, we conducted a series of 21 engaging live sessions to showcase the thrilling innovations included in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience–anywhere, anytime.

 

 

 Find All of Our Assets   

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Step-by-Step Instructions for Effortless Electronic Documents Implementation with TCI
entry.content:
Earlier this month SAP announced the release of TCI (Transport-Based Correction Instructions) for electronic document processing. In this blog post we will show how to use TCI to implement or update the Electronic Document Processing Framework – also known as SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (SAP DRC).

What is TCI?

SAP Note Transport-Based Correction Instructions is a method to ship ABAP correction instructions in a flexible manner. It bundles the corrections into packages, making it easier to implement at once. The complete overview of TCI is available in SAP Note 2187425 - Information about SAP Note Transport based Correction Instructions (TCI), along with all the pre-requisites of the functionality.

What are the benefits of using TCI?

Implementing SAP Notes manually is a time consuming task. With TCI the implementation of the Electronic Document Framework can be sped up. Other than the time saved, TCI has a wide range of benefits including but not limited to:

  • No developer skills needed for implementation
  • No adjustment activities during SP import and upgrade for SAP standard objects
  • Option to de-implement or roll back TCI
  • Support of all tranport-enabled SAP ABAP objects

How to use TCI to implement Electronic Document Processing Framework?

To confirm the TCI enablement is active, open transaction SNOTE. In the menu check if option Goto -> Upload TCI exists.

Once the TCI is enabled follow the steps below to implement the Electronic Document Processing Framework:

  1. Download the SAR file from the SAP Note. As of writing this blog the TCI is available for version 36 of the Electronic Document Processing Framework in SAP Note 3545430 - Transport-Based Correction Instructions (TCI) for the Electronic Document Processing Framework (1 to 36).  
    1. Open the SAP Note in SAP for Me and select Correction Instructions.
      Note: In this example we have used an SAP S/4HANA On-Premise 2020 system.



    2. Select the Software Component matching the system.
      1. To check the software components of the system go to the menu and select System -> Status.
      2. In the new window press Details.
      3. In the second window look for the software component S4CORE or SAP_APPL. Whichever is present in the system, the corresponding one should be selected in SAP for Me Correction Instructions.

    3. Select the Version matching the system, and press Download on the right hand side.

       

  2. Upload the SAR archive to the SAP system. Open transaction SNOTE, and select Upload TCI.


  3. Upload the TCI Note to the system. In transaction SNOTE select Upload SAP Note. If downloading Digitally Signed SAP Note is enabled in the system, select Download SAP Note instead.


  4. Implement the TCI Note. In transaction SNOTE enter the TCI Note then select SAP Note -> Implement SAP Note.

With that Electronic Document Processing Framework is successfully implemented in the system.

If the corrections are to be shipped to other systems in the landscape, release and import the created transport request to the other systems.

Note: If multiple transports were created, import them in one queue.

entry.title: Introduction to SAP DRC and Integration aspects
entry.content:
SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (SAP DRC) enables the organization to create, process, and monitor electronic documents and statutory reports. As a result, the company stays compliant with local legal obligations.

In this blog I will refer this as DRC or SAP DRC

Core Features of SAP DRC are

  1. E-Invoices for Sales
  2. Incoming Invoices
  3. Transport Registrations
  4. Legal/Statutory reporting based on country requirements

                 

Core features (in detail)

  • SAP DRC is offered on premise and on cloud and the integration with the tax authorities is standard content delivered by SAP.
  • Transport Registration is mandatory in some countries like Portugal, Romania etc so this feature is supported for relevant countires
  • Tax reporting is supported for most of the countries (NOT ALL) so ensure you check this link before assuming – SAP HELP
  • SAP DRC on Cloud license is based on volumes of invoices per month along with the countries in scope for an organization. Details can only be validated with SAP account manager.

Solution brief

Now we will discuss the process flow for both outgoing and incoming invoices

Outgoing (Sales) invoice Processing

E-Doc Cockpit view (Transaction EDOC_COCKPIT)

XML view

Transport Order generation

Incoming (purchase) invoice Processing

Statutory/Legal Reporting

Country specific reports can be generated from SAP DRC. These can be

  • VAT reports
  • SAF-T reports
  • EC Sales list
  • Many more based on country requirements

Process flow

Relevant Fiori apps

Is it on Premise or on cloud?

  1. In the business systems to make compliance a seamless step within the business processes, with no data redundancy and no data replication. This is On-Premises
  1. On SAP Business Technology Platform to connect to govt authorities and platforms. This can be SAP DRC Cloud.

For more detailed insights on SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) and the cloud edition, below links can be referred

  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition – Online Help Documentation
  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition and SAP S/4HANA – Onlin...
  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition – Online Help Documentat...
  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for SAP ERP
  • Overview video: Integration with Peppol network with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud ed...
  • Overview video: How can SAP Document and Reporting Compliance support you in meeting the new KSeF re...
  • Overview video: Exchange invoices over the Peppol network using the Cloud Edition and Concur Invoice
  • Overview video: Discover the New eOrdering Process of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

I will share more details around country specifics in my next blog, till then enjoy reading and share your experience in comments

(some images are taken from other SAP content and the copyright remains with the owner)
